note: Benchmark targets (spec 24, design §19.2). A SCORED target carries `expected` ground truth and measures false-NEGATIVES/recall; a CLEAN target (no `expected`) measures the false-POSITIVE rate. The seeded vuln-app's weaknesses are SAST-detectable from source (--source), so it is scored even though the live app (examples/vuln-app/server.js, port 3000) crashes on DB routes. Run it with `node examples/vuln-app/server.js` (needs `npm i express` there), or point url at any reachable in-scope host. Edit URLs to match your environment.

holdout.note: K-fold (oracle.kfold) over SCORED targets only. The §19 proposer trains on the train fold; a champion is promoted ONLY on improvement over the HELD-OUT fold it never trained on (the antidote to benchmark overfitting). A genuine split needs >=2 scored targets — add reachable, ground-truthed targets below as the living corpus grows.

targets.name: juice-shop
targets.note: OWASP Juice Shop (`make up`). Black-box scored target — ground truth at bench/expected/juice-shop.json (13 documented challenges across 7 catalog classes). The 2nd SCORED target: with seeded-vuln-app this makes holdout.k=2 a genuine held-out split (oracle.kfold) and clears the §19.2/D16 oracle-adequacy gate for live HC auto-adoption.
